     The Numbers Business Report

Argo (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $136,025,503Details
International Box Office $91,115,254
Worldwide Box Office $227,140,757
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $25,882,405
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $19,092,430
Total $44,974,835
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On November 4, 1979, as the Iranian revolution reaches its boiling point, militants storm the U.S. embassy in Tehran, taking 52 Americans hostage. But, in the midst of the chaos, six Americans manage to slip away and find refuge in the home of the Canadian ambassador. Knowing it is only a matter of time before the six are found out and likely killed, a CIA “exfiltration” specialist named Tony Mendez comes up with a risky plan to get them safely out of the country. A plan so incredible, it could only happen in the movies.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,458,109 (14.3% of total gross)
Legs:6.99 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:59.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$44,500,000 (worldwide box office is 5.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,232 opening theaters/3,247 max. theaters, 9.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $203,522,172

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 12th, 2012 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: October 11th, 2012 (Wide) (Thailand)
October 17th, 2012 (Wide) (Philippines)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Chile)
October 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Colombia)
Video Release: February 19th, 2013 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language and some violent images.
(Rating bulletin 2220, 4/25/2012)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Director Winner, BAFTA Best Film Winner, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Director, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Picture, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Director—Motion Picture, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner, Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Plot point: False Identity, Hostage, Political, Rescue, Secret Agent, Undercover
Social setting: Movie Business, Rebellion
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., GK Films, Smoke House Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Farsi

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

On November 4, 1979, as the Iranian revolution reaches its boiling point, militants storm the U.S. embassy in Tehran, taking 52 Americans hostage. But, in the midst of the chaos, six Americans manage to slip away and find refuge in the home of the Canadian ambassador. Knowing it is only a matter of time before the six are found out and likely killed, a CIA “exfiltration” specialist named Tony Mendez comes up with a risky plan to get them safely out of the country. A plan so incredible, it could only happen in the movies.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,458,109 (14.3% of total gross)
Legs:6.99 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:59.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$44,500,000 (worldwide box office is 5.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,232 opening theaters/3,247 max. theaters, 9.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $203,522,172

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 12th, 2012 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: October 11th, 2012 (Wide) (Thailand)
October 17th, 2012 (Wide) (Philippines)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Chile)
October 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Colombia)
Video Release: February 19th, 2013 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language and some violent images.
(Rating bulletin 2220, 4/25/2012)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Director Winner, BAFTA Best Film Winner, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Director, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Picture, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Director—Motion Picture, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner, Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Plot point: False Identity, Hostage, Political, Rescue, Secret Agent, Undercover
Social setting: Movie Business, Rebellion
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., GK Films, Smoke House Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Farsi
